title: Error states, loading polish, accessibility hardening, and validation improvements
description: PRD-36 addresses five areas of polish and hardening: (1) Calendar page error state so users get clear feedback when event loading fails, (2) Dashboard stats loading skeleton for a jank-free initial load, (3) FilterBar comprehensive ARIA labels for screen-reader users, (4) Bulk delete input validation and partial-failure reporting on the backend, (5) DetailPanel loading/error/pending state tests to prevent regressions.

userStories.title: Calendar page should display error feedback when event loading fails
userStories.description: Calendar.jsx handles isLoading but has no error state. When useCalendarEvents() returns an error the user sees a blank page with no feedback. Add an ErrorState render when error is truthy so users understand the failure and can retry.
acceptanceCriteria: When useCalendarEvents returns an error, Calendar page shows an ErrorState component with a descriptive message and retry button
Error state does not appear during normal load — only when error is truthy
Loading skeleton still renders while isLoading is true
Test added: 'should show error state when calendar event loading fails'
userStories.rationale: Silent failures destroy user trust. Without an error state users assume the app is broken or their events were deleted. A clear error message with retry is the minimum viable feedback loop.

userStories.title: Dashboard stats section should show loading skeleton instead of empty cards during initial load
userStories.description: Dashboard.jsx renders stats cards during load but doesn't conditionally show a loading skeleton, causing a flash of empty/zero values. Add a shimmer skeleton while useApplicationStats isLoading is true so users see intentional feedback instead of incorrect data.
acceptanceCriteria: StatsBar or stats section shows 4 animated skeleton cards while isLoading is true
Once data arrives, skeleton is replaced by real values without layout shift
Existing stats tests still pass
Test added: 'should render loading skeleton while stats are loading'
userStories.rationale: Empty stats flash makes users think they have no tracked applications. A loading skeleton sets the correct expectation and gives a more polished feel on first load.

userStories.title: FilterBar should have complete ARIA labels and semantic structure for screen readers
userStories.description: FilterBar.jsx lacks aria-label on checkbox groups, no aria-live region for filter change announcements, and some inputs lack associated labels via htmlFor. Improve accessibility so screen reader users can discover, navigate, and use filters without confusion.
acceptanceCriteria: Main filter container has role='region' and aria-label='Filter Controls'
Each checkbox group has an aria-label describing what it filters (e.g. 'Filter by status')
An aria-live='polite' region announces the active filter count when filters change
All checkboxes have associated <label> elements via htmlFor/id pairing
No visual regressions — ARIA changes are additive
Automated test checks aria-label presence on key filter groups
userStories.rationale: Screen reader users cannot effectively filter their applications without proper ARIA semantics. This is a legal accessibility requirement (WCAG 2.1 AA) and directly impacts inclusivity.

userStories.title: Bulk delete endpoint should validate input array size and report partial failures
userStories.description: The bulk delete endpoint accepts an unbounded array of IDs with no max-size validation. A malicious or buggy client could pass 10,000+ IDs, causing OOM or timeout. Add a BulkDeleteRequest schema with a max-length constraint and update the response to include deleted_ids and failed_ids so callers can handle partial failures.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/applications/bulk-delete rejects requests with more than 500 IDs (422 response)
POST /api/applications/bulk-delete rejects empty arrays (422 response)
Successful response body includes { deleted_count, failed_ids } fields
Backend test: 'test_bulk_delete_rejects_oversized_request' (>500 IDs → 422)
Backend test: 'test_bulk_delete_rejects_empty_array' ([] → 422)
Existing bulk delete happy-path tests still pass
userStories.rationale: Unbounded array inputs are a DoS vector. Adding max-length validation is a one-line Pydantic constraint that prevents resource exhaustion and gives callers actionable failure data.

userStories.title: DetailPanel tests should cover loading, error, and mutation-pending states
userStories.description: DetailPanel.jsx is the primary interaction surface for editing applications but its tests lack coverage for async states. Add tests for: loading spinner while data fetches, error message when fetch fails, save button disabled while mutation is pending, and success feedback after save.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: 'should display loading state while fetching application detail'
Test: 'should display error state when application detail fetch fails'
Test: 'should disable save actions while mutation is in progress'
All new tests use MSW handlers or mock hook return values — no direct fetch mocks
Tests follow Arrange → Act → Assert structure with blank lines between sections
No existing tests broken by additions
userStories.rationale: DetailPanel regressions are high-impact: any breakage affects every application edit workflow. Without async-state tests, loading/error/pending UI can silently break without CI catching it.
